summaries.title: Antecedents and correlates of improved cognitive performance in children exposed in utero to low levels of lead.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the study used clinical measurements, blood samples, and standardized cognitive scales from a prospective cohort.
summaries.summary: A prospective study investigating the relationship between prenatal lead exposure and cognitive development in children from 24 to 57 months of age, identifying factors that facilitate cognitive recovery or compensation.
key_findings: High prenatal lead exposure (10-254 µg/dL) is associated with significantly lower cognitive scores at 2 years of age.
Children appear to recover from or compensate for early lead insults by age 5.
Recovery is associated with lower blood lead levels at 57 months, higher socioeconomic status, higher HOME scores, higher maternal IQ, and female gender.
Risk of persistent cognitive deficit is increased in children with high prenatal exposure and either high postnatal exposure or less optimal sociodemographic characteristics.
summaries.population: 170 children (median age 57.8 months)
limitations: specific to a single clinical cohort

summaries.title: Semiparametric modeling of age at achieving developmental milestones after prenatal exposure to methylmercury in the Seychelles child development study.

key_findings: Very slight delays (<1 day) in walking were seen at mercury levels 0-7 ppm, but this effect did not persist at higher exposure levels.
No evidence for any association between prenatal exposure and age at talking.
captured_features: age at first saying words other than 'mama' or 'dada'
summaries.population: 779 Seychelloian children
limitations: difficult to conclude cause-and-effect relations at low exposure levels

summaries.ehr_evidence: Information on pregnancy outcome was obtained from the Medical Birth Registry of Norway.
summaries.summary: A prospective cohort study evaluating the risk of selected birth defects associated with maternal exposure to non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) during the first 12 weeks of pregnancy.
key_findings: No associations were observed between overall exposure to NSAIDs during pregnancy and the selected birth defects (adjusted odds ratio 0.7, 95% confidence interval 0.4–1.1).
No associations were found between maternal use of specific types of NSAIDs and the selected birth defects.
summaries.population: 69,929 women in Norway (1999-2006)
limitations: small numbers of NSAID-exposed infants for individual birth defect categories

summaries.title: An integrated, multistudy analysis of the safety of Ann Arbor strain live attenuated influenza vaccine in children aged 2-17 years.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study is an integrated analysis of randomized controlled trials (RCTs), not electronic health records.
summaries.summary: An integrated analysis of multiple randomized controlled trials to evaluate the safety and reactogenicity of the Ann Arbor strain live attenuated influenza vaccine (LAIV) in children aged 2–17 years.
key_findings: LAIV recipients exhibited increased runny/stuffy nose, headache, and tiredness/decreased activity compared to placebo.
No evidence of an increase in any potential vaccine-related serious adverse events.
No evidence that lower respiratory illness or wheezing illness occurred at a higher rate in LAIV recipients.
summaries.study_design: Integrated analysis of randomized controlled trials
summaries.population: Children aged 2–17 years
limitations: Integrated analysis of existing trials rather than a single prospective cohort

summaries.title: The influence of BCG vaccine strain on mycobacteria-specific and non-specific immune responses in a prospective cohort of infants in Uganda.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None. The study is a prospective cohort study.
summaries.summary: Evaluation of specific and non-specific immune responses to different BCG vaccine strains (Russia, Bulgaria, Denmark) in a cohort of infants in Uganda.
key_findings: BCG-Denmark immunised infants showed the highest cytokine responses.
Scar frequency differed significantly by strain (Russia 52.2%, Bulgaria 64.1%, Denmark 92.6%).
Scarred infants had higher IFN-gamma and IL-13 responses to mycobacterial antigens.
BCG-Denmark group had the highest frequency of adverse events.
summaries.population: infants in Uganda

summaries.title: Poor maternal nutrition programmes a pro-atherosclerotic phenotype in ApoE-/- mice.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None. The study uses an experimental animal model (ApoE− / − mice).
summaries.summary: The study investigates the effects of maternal low-protein (LP) diet during pregnancy and lactation on the development of atherosclerosis in ApoE− / − mouse offspring.
key_findings: LP offspring showed significantly greater plaque area (P < 0.05)
increased cholesterol clefts
significantly higher indices of DNA damage
increased expression of HMG-CoA reductase and LDL receptor in the liver
higher LDL-cholesterol levels
trend towards elevated insulin
summaries.population: Male offspring of pregnant dams

summaries.title: Maternal Distress during Pregnancy and Offspring Childhood Overweight.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Data were obtained via computerized telephone interviews and web-based/posted questionnaires from the Danish National Birth Cohort.
summaries.summary: A prospective cohort study investigating whether maternal distress (anxiety, depression, and stress) during pregnancy is associated with childhood overweight in offspring at age 7.
key_findings: Prenatal exposure to maternal distress was not associated with childhood overweight at 7 years of age (adjusted OR 1.06, 95% CI 0.96-1.18).
A small tendency of overweight was seen in boys (OR 1.15, 0.99-1.33) but not in girls (OR 0.98, 0.85-1.13).
summaries.population: 37,764 women and child dyads from the Danish National Birth Cohort

summaries.title: Childhood brain tumours and use of mobile phones: comparison of a case-control study with incidence data.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Swedish Cancer registry
summaries.summary: Commentary on the CEFALO case-control study regarding mobile phone use and brain tumour risk in children, highlighting the inconsistency between case-control results and stable incidence trends in registries.
key_findings: No clear exposure-response relationship found.
Incidence trends for brain tumours in children remain stable despite increased mobile phone use.
Potential risk may be restricted to heavy users or very early life exposure.
summaries.population: children and adolescents

summaries.title: Pilot study of pesticide knowledge, attitudes, and practices among pregnant women in northern Thailand.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: A pilot study evaluating pesticide knowledge, attitudes, and practices among 76 pregnant women in northern Thailand to identify factors associated with prenatal pesticide exposure.
key_findings: Lower knowledge scores and early pregnancy trimesters were marginally associated with unsafe home pesticide practices.
Women who worked in agriculture, applied pesticides before pregnancy, or had a previous child were significantly more likely to engage in unsafe home pesticide behaviors.
summaries.population: 76 pregnant women in northern Thailand
summaries.data_availability_statement: Supplementary File 1:PDF-Document (PDF, 721 KB)

summaries.title: Early metabolic defects in dexamethasone-exposed and undernourished intrauterine growth restricted rats.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: Study investigating early metabolic defects in rat pups with intrauterine growth restriction (IUGR) induced by dexamethasone exposure or prenatal undernutrition.
key_findings: DEX and UN IUGR pups presented basal hyperglycemia, decreased glucose tolerance, and pancreatic islet atrophy.
DEX pups presented decreased insulin sensitivity.
UN pups exhibited lowered glucose-induced insulin secretion and more marked alterations in gene expression of pancreatic islet and adipose tissue development regulators.
summaries.population: IUGR rat pups

summaries.title: Assessment of prenatal exposure to arsenic in Tenerife Island.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used questionnaires and meconium samples, not electronic health records.
summaries.summary: A transversal descriptive study assessing prenatal arsenic exposure via meconium analysis in 96 mother-child pairs in Tenerife, Spain, and its association with maternal diet and lifestyle.
key_findings: Arsenic was detected in 38.5% of meconium samples.
Frequent intake of vegetables was associated with detectable meconium arsenic levels.
No association was found between arsenic exposure and neonatal effects.
summaries.population: 96 mother-child pairs in Tenerife, Spain

summaries.title: Maternal and offspring fasting glucose and type 2 diabetes-associated genetic variants and cognitive function at age 8: a Mendelian randomization study in the Avon Longitudinal Study of Parents and Children.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses the Avon Longitudinal Study of Parents and Children (ALSPAC) cohort, which is a birth cohort study, not an EHR.
summaries.summary: A Mendelian randomization study investigating whether maternal and offspring genetic risk scores for fasting glucose and type 2 diabetes are causally linked to child intelligence quotient (IQ) at age 8.
key_findings: No association found between maternal or offspring fasting glucose genetic risk scores (FGGRS) and child IQ.
Maternal type 2 diabetes genetic risk score (T2DGRS) was positively associated with offspring IQ.
Suggests no causal link between in utero glucose exposure and childhood IQ.
summaries.population: Children at age 8 in the ALSPAC cohort

summaries.title: Prenatal p,p´-DDE exposure and neurodevelopment among children 3.5-5 years of age.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study is a prospective perinatal cohort study using direct data collection (interviews, blood samples, and monitoring) rather than EHR.
summaries.summary: A prospective perinatal cohort study in Morelos, Mexico, investigating the association between prenatal DDE exposure and neurodevelopmental outcomes in children aged 3.5–5 years.
key_findings: The study evaluates neurodevelopmental outcomes at 42, 48, 54, and 60 months of age based on prenatal DDE exposure.
captured_features: McCarthy’s Scales of Children’s Abilities scores
summaries.population: Children aged 3.5–5 years in Morelos, Mexico
limitations: The provided text is incomplete and does not list specific limitations.

summaries.title: Prospective surveillance study of acute respiratory infections, influenza-like illness and seasonal influenza vaccine in a cohort of juvenile idiopathic arthritis patients.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study was a prospective surveillance study in a tertiary Pediatric Rheumatology Clinic.
summaries.summary: Prospective surveillance study evaluating the frequency of acute respiratory infections (ARI) and influenza-like illness (ILI) in juvenile idiopathic arthritis (JIA) patients, their impact on disease activity, and the immunogenicity/safety of the influenza vaccine.
key_findings: 105 ARI episodes reported
20% of JIA flares were temporally associated with ARI
Influenza seroprotection rates > 70%
Influenza vaccine was immunogenic and safe in JIA patients
summaries.population: Juvenile idiopathic arthritis (JIA) patients attending a tertiary Pediatric Rheumatology Clinic

summaries.title: Clonal expansion within pneumococcal serotype 6C after use of seven-valent vaccine.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses whole-genome sequences of 19 isolates rather than EHR or linked health data.
summaries.summary: Genomic analysis of S. pneumoniae serotype 6C isolates to investigate clonal expansion and genetic plasticity following the introduction of the seven-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (PCV7).
key_findings: Four broad phylogenetically distinct lineages were identified.
Rapid clonal expansion occurred within a single MLST clonal complex (CC395).
Plasticity exists within current and potential vaccine-associated loci.
summaries.study_design: Genomic analysis and phylogenetic network construction
summaries.population: Pediatric and adult population (implied by vaccine context)

summaries.title: Environmental factors predicting blood lead levels in pregnant women in the UK: the ALSPAC study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Data were collected via self-completion postal questionnaires and whole blood samples.
summaries.summary: A study identifying factors contributing to maternal blood lead levels (BLL) in a large cohort of pregnant women in the UK.
key_findings: Higher educational qualification was a strong independent predictor of higher BLL.
Cigarette smoking, alcohol and coffee drinking, and heating the home with a coal fire were predictive factors for higher BLL.
Iron and calcium intake showed evidence of protective effects against higher BLL.
summaries.population: Pregnant women resident in the Avon area of the UK

summaries.title: Exposure to the Chinese famine in early life and the risk of anaemia in adulthood.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The data are based on a subsample from Jiangsu province... from the 2002 Chinese National Nutrition and Health Survey.
summaries.summary: This study investigated the association between early life exposure to the Chinese famine (1959–1961) and the risk of anaemia in adulthood using data from a national nutrition and health survey.
key_findings: Fetal exposure to the Chinese famine was associated with a 37% increased risk of anaemia in adulthood (RR 1.37).
The association was significantly stronger among men (RR 1.87), overweight or obese individuals (RR 1.75), and those with lower educational levels (RR 2.07).
captured_features: famine exposure timing (fetal, early-childhood, mid-childhood, late-childhood)
summaries.population: 2007 adults born between 1954 and 1964 in Jiangsu province, China
limitations: Potential recall bias regarding famine exposure

summaries.title: Monitoring the immune response to vaccination with an inactivated vaccine associated to bovine neonatal pancytopenia by deep sequencing transcriptome analysis in cattle.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses next-generation RNA sequencing (RNAseq) on blood samples from cattle, not electronic health records.
summaries.summary: A study using RNAseq to analyze the blood transcriptome of cattle vaccinated with a BVDV vaccine associated with bovine neonatal pancytopenia (BNP).
key_findings: Identified a novel cytokine-like gene highly upregulated after vaccination.
Revealed a coordinated immune response to dsRNA or dsRNA analogue after vaccination with an inactivated ssRNA vaccine.
summaries.population: Cattle (calves)
limitations: Specific to a single vaccine type

summaries.title: Deep phenotyping of the unselected COPSAC2010 birth cohort study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study describes a prospective clinical cohort where data is collected during clinic visits and biobanking, rather than from an existing EHR database.
summaries.summary: The COPSAC 2010 birth cohort study investigates the interaction between perinatal exposures (microbiome, maternal nutrition) and genetic factors in the development of chronic inflammatory diseases like asthma in children.
key_findings: High adherence rate (95-98%) for clinic visits.
Recruitment of 738 mothers and 700 children.
Establishment of a longitudinal framework for deep phenotyping and biobanking.
summaries.study_design: Prospective birth cohort study with clinical follow-up and randomized controlled trials
summaries.population: Pregnant women and their offspring (children up to age 3) in eastern Denmark
summaries.exposure_domain: human microbiome, maternal nutrition (vitamin D, fish oil), perinatal exposures

summaries.title: Progress in immunization information systems--United States, 2011.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The text mentions interoperability with electronic health records as a functionality of IIS, but the data analyzed comes from the IIS Annual Report (IISAR) survey.
summaries.summary: A report on the progress of Immunization Information Systems (IIS) in the United States in 2011, evaluating child participation and the completeness of core data elements.
key_findings: 84% of U.S. children <6 years participated in an IIS in 2011.
59% of grantees included all 18 core data elements.
Average completeness of core data elements ranged from 38% to ≥98%.
summaries.study_design: Descriptive analysis of administrative survey data
summaries.population: Children <6 years in the United States

summaries.title: Selenium protects neonates against neurotoxicity from prenatal exposure to manganese.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used direct measurements of umbilical cord serum concentrations and Neonatal Behavioral Neurological Assessment (NBNA) tests rather than EHR.
summaries.summary: An epidemiological study investigating the protective effect of Selenium (Se) against Manganese (Mn)-induced neurotoxicity in neonates in Shanghai, China.
key_findings: Mn was negatively associated with NBNA scores at low Se levels (adjusted OR = 5.7).
The adverse effect of Mn was significantly reduced at high Se levels.
Mn/Se ratio was moderately correlated with NBNA scores.
summaries.population: 933 mother-newborn pairs in Shanghai, China
limitations: Not explicitly stated in the provided text

summaries.title: Prenatal exposure to organophosphate pesticides and neurobehavioral development of neonates: a birth cohort study in Shenyang, China.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; data were obtained via questionnaires and urine analysis.
summaries.summary: A birth cohort study in Shenyang, China, investigating the association between prenatal organophosphate pesticide (OP) exposure and neonatal neurobehavioral development.
key_findings: Prenatal total DAP concentrations were inversely associated with scores on the NBNA scales.
A 10-fold increase in DAP concentrations was associated with a decrease of 1.78 regarding the Summary NBNA.
There was an estimated 2.11-point difference in summary NBNA scores between neonates in the highest quintile of prenatal OP exposure and the lowest quintile group.
captured_features: Urinary DAP metabolites (DMP, DMTP, DEP, DETP)
summaries.population: 249 pregnant women and their neonates in Shenyang, China

summaries.title: Long-term effects of maternal deprivation on cholinergic system in rat brain.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses an animal model (Wistar rats) and laboratory measurements, not electronic health records.
summaries.summary: The study investigates the long-term effects of maternal deprivation (MD) on the cholinergic system in the brains of Wistar rats, specifically measuring acetylcholinesterase (AChE) activity and cholinergic fiber density in the hippocampus and retrosplenial (RS) cortex.
key_findings: Significant decrease of AChE activity in the cortex and increase in the hippocampus of MD rats.
Density of cholinergic fibers significantly increased in CA1 region of hippocampus and decreased in RS cortex.
summaries.population: Male Wistar rats

summaries.title: Determining prenatal, early childhood and cumulative long-term lead exposure using micro-spatial deciduous dentine levels.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses biological samples (blood, bone, and teeth) from a prospective cohort, not EHR.
summaries.summary: Validation of micro-spatial dentine lead (Pb) levels as a biomarker for estimating prenatal, early childhood, and cumulative long-term lead exposure.
key_findings: Dentine Pb at birth significantly associated with cord blood Pb (r=0.69).
Prenatal dentine Pb associated with maternal patella and tibia Pb.
Dentine Pb at 3 months associated with childhood blood Pb (r=0.64).
Secondary dentine Pb correlated with cumulative blood lead index (r=0.38).
summaries.population: Children (3 months to 6 years) and mothers in a prospective pregnancy cohort

summaries.title: Prenatal exposure to maternal bereavement and childbirths in the offspring: a population-based cohort study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Data is stored at the secure platform of Denmark Statistics, which is the central authority on Danish statistics.
summaries.summary: A population-based cohort study investigating the association between prenatal exposure to maternal bereavement and offspring fertility in Denmark and Sweden.
key_findings: No overall association between prenatal exposure to maternal stress and having a child in early adulthood.
Females born to mothers who lost a parent had a slightly reduced probability of having children (HR 0.97).
Females born to mothers who lost a child (HR 1.09), spouse (HR 1.29), or sibling (HR 1.13) had an increased probability of having children.
summaries.population: subjects born in Denmark after 1968 and in Sweden after 1973
limitations: longer time of follow-up is necessary in order to reach a firmer conclusion
summaries.data_availability_statement: The authors confirm that, for approved reasons, some access restrictions apply to the data underlying the findings. All data is stored at the secure platform of Denmark Statistics, which is the central authority on Danish statistics with the mission to collect, compile and publish statistics on the Danish society. By legislations, researchers who want to get access to the data must apply for permissions from Both Statistics Denmark and Danish Data Protection Agency. Anyone with interests in this data can contact the authors of the study.

summaries.title: Quantity and diversity of environmental microbial exposure and development of asthma: a birth cohort study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the study uses a birth cohort with questionnaires and dust samples.
summaries.summary: A prospective birth cohort study of 410 children in Finland investigating the association between environmental microbial exposure (quantity and diversity) and the development of asthma, wheezing, and atopic dermatitis up to age 6.
key_findings: Total quantity of microbial exposure (sum of indicators for fungi, Gram-positive, and Gram-negative bacteria) was significantly associated with asthma incidence in an inverted-U shape (highest risk at medium levels).
Microbial diversity score was inversely associated with the risk of wheezing.
Microbial diversity score was significantly associated with sensitization to inhalant allergens in an inverted-U shape.
summaries.population: 410 children born in Eastern and Middle Finland (2002-2005)
limitations: Only few associations found between single microbial markers
Need for better indicators of total quantity and diversity of microbial exposure

summaries.title: An unusual case of vaccine-associated paralytic poliomyelitis.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: A case report of a child who developed vaccine-associated paralytic poliomyelitis after receiving oral polio vaccine (OPV) in China.
key_findings: Child developed flaccid paralysis after OPV
MRI and EMG confirmed poliomyelitis
Stool culture showed poliovirus type 3
summaries.population: one child (almost one year old)

summaries.title: Altered cortical thickness following prenatal sodium valproate exposure.
summaries.ehr_evidence: No mention of EHR or linked health data; participants were recruited via hospital and community advertisements for a prospective study.
summaries.summary: A study investigating the effects of prenatal sodium valproate (VPA) exposure on cortical thickness in children using MRI and neuropsychological assessments.
key_findings: Increased left inferior frontal gyrus (IFG) and left pericalcarine sulcus thickness in VPA-exposed children.
Association between VPA dose and right IFG thickness.
Absence of normal asymmetry in the IFG region of VPA-exposed children.
Relationship between verbal skills and left IFG thickness.
summaries.population: Children (mean age 7.2-8.1 years)

summaries.title: Prenatal organochlorine and methylmercury exposure and memory and learning in school-age children in communities near the New Bedford Harbor Superfund site, Massachusetts.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: The study assesses the association between low-level prenatal exposure to PCBs, organochlorines, and methylmercury and memory and learning in school-age children living near the New Bedford Harbor Superfund Site.
summaries.population: school-age children in communities near the New Bedford Harbor Superfund Site, Massachusetts

summaries.title: Prenatal exposure to arsenic and cadmium impacts infectious disease-related genes within the glucocorticoid receptor signal transduction pathway.
summaries.ehr_evidence: No mention of EHR or linked health data in the provided text.
summaries.summary: A compilation of research titles exploring the impacts of prenatal and early-life arsenic and cadmium exposure on infant health, including birth weight, immune system development, and epigenetic modifications.
key_findings: Arsenic exposure is associated with lower birth weight and increased risk of respiratory infections.
Cadmium exposure is linked to altered thymus and spleen cell phenotypes.
Prenatal arsenic exposure is associated with altered DNA methylation in cord blood.
summaries.study_design: Prospective cohort studies and laboratory experiments
summaries.population: Infants, newborns, and pregnant women in Bangladesh, USA, Chile, and India
limitations: The input text is a list of titles rather than a single cohesive manuscript, making it difficult to extract specific methodology.

summaries.title: Prenatal exposure to endocrine disrupting chemicals in relation to thyroid hormone levels in infants - a Dutch prospective cohort study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Information on T4 levels in heel prick blood spots was obtained through the neonatal screening programme in the Netherlands.
summaries.summary: A Dutch prospective cohort study assessing the association between prenatal exposure to various endocrine disrupting chemicals (EDCs) and thyroxine (T4) levels in newborns.
key_findings: Girls in the highest quartile of DDE and PFOA exposure showed an increased T4 level.
Boys in the second quartile of exposure for both PFOS and PFOA showed a lower T4 level, though this was attenuated after full adjustment.
No effects were observed for other compounds.
summaries.population: newborns in a mother-child cohort in the Netherlands (n=83)

summaries.title: Interim CDC guidance for polio vaccination for travel to and from countries affected by wild poliovirus.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: Public health guidance and vaccine recommendations for travelers to countries with wild poliovirus (WPV) circulation, including specific schedules for infants and children.
key_findings: CDC and WHO provide updated vaccination requirements for travelers to polio-endemic or affected countries.
Infants and children in the US should receive 4 doses of IPV.
summaries.population: Travelers (infants and children)

summaries.title: Notes from the field: rotavirus vaccine administration errors--United States, 2006-2013.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used reports from the Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System (VAERS).
summaries.summary: Analysis of rotavirus vaccine administration errors, specifically injection errors and eye splashes, in the United States between 2006 and 2013.
key_findings: 66 reports of administration errors identified
39 reports of injection errors
27 reports of eye splashes
Common reasons for errors included misinterpreting package inserts, confusing syringes/vials, and inadequate training
summaries.population: Infants and children in the United States
limitations: Passive surveillance system may capture only a small fraction of errors

summaries.title: Reactogenicity and safety of the human rotavirus vaccine, Rotarix™ in The Philippines, Sri Lanka, and India: a post-marketing surveillance study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None mentioned; the study is described as a post-marketing surveillance study.
summaries.summary: A post-marketing surveillance study evaluating the reactogenicity and safety of the Rotarix™ rotavirus vaccine in infants in The Philippines, Sri Lanka, and India.
key_findings: 14.7% of infants in the Philippines, 14.9% in Sri Lanka, and 12.7% in India recorded at least Grade 2/3 solicited adverse events.
Most common solicited adverse events were irritability (Philippines and India) and fever (Sri Lanka).
41 serious adverse events (SAEs) were recorded in the Philippines, with 6 considered causally related to vaccination.
One vaccine-unrelated SAE occurred in a Sri Lankan infant.
summaries.population: infants in The Philippines, Sri Lanka, and India
limitations: Not specified in the provided text

summaries.title: Gender-specific mortality in DTP-IPV- and MMR±MenC-eligible age groups to determine possible sex-differential effects of vaccination: an observational study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None mentioned
summaries.summary: An observational study investigating whether there are gender-specific differences in mortality among Dutch children based on the type of last vaccination received (DTP-IPV vs. MMR ± MenC).
key_findings: No significant differences in gender-specific mortality related to the type of last vaccination received were observed.
Mortality for natural causes was higher among boys than girls in all age groups.
Adjusted IRRs for girls compared with boys ranged between 0.81 and 0.91.
summaries.population: Dutch children aged 0-11 years (2000-2011)
limitations: inability to detect small non-specific effects if they were not reflected in mortality differences

summaries.title: In vitro and in vivo growth alter the population dynamic and properties of a Jeryl Lynn mumps vaccine.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: A laboratory study investigating the genetic and phenotypic differences between two mumps vaccines (JL-CK and RIT4385) produced in different cell substrates (canine kidney cells vs. chicken embryo fibroblasts).
key_findings: JL-CK vaccine contains a unique mix of mumps viruses with mutations acquired in canine kidney cells.
Growth in cell or animal substrates dramatically alters the population dynamic of JL-CK.
JL-CK showed a higher degree of hydrocephalus formation in neonatal rat brains compared to RIT4385.
Only 4% of the JL-CK population corresponded to the parental Jeryl Lynn-5 strain.
summaries.study_design: In vitro and in vivo laboratory study
summaries.population: Cell lines (Vero, MDCK) and neonatal rat brains
limitations: Laboratory study, not human clinical data
summaries.data_availability_statement: Associated Data Supplementary Materials

summaries.title: Perinatal Exposure to Traffic-Related Air Pollution and Atopy at 1 Year of Age in a Multi-Center Canadian Birth Cohort Study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study is a birth cohort study (CHILD study) and does not mention the use of electronic health records.
summaries.summary: A multi-center Canadian birth cohort study investigating the association between perinatal exposure to traffic-related air pollution (TRAP) and atopy in children at 1 year of age.
key_findings: The study aims to assess the association between perinatal TRAP exposure and atopy at 1 year of age (results not fully detailed in the provided snippet).
summaries.population: Canadian infants
limitations: Limitations of land use regression (LUR) models in capturing fine-scale seasonal variability

summaries.title: Association between levels of persistent organic pollutants in adipose tissue and cryptorchidism in early childhood: a case-control study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used subcutaneous adipose tissue biopsies taken during surgery, not electronic health records.
summaries.summary: A case-control study investigating the association between prenatal exposure to persistent organic pollutants (PCBs, PCDD/Fs, and PBDEs) in adipose tissue and the risk of cryptorchidism in children.
key_findings: Total-TEq (dioxins and dioxin-like PCBs) and sum of PCDD/Fs were positively associated with cryptorchidism (OR 3.21 and 3.69, respectively).
Sum of PCBs was close to significant (OR 1.92).
Sum of PBDEs was not significantly associated (OR 0.86).
summaries.population: children (44 cryptorchid cases, 38 controls) in Finland and Denmark

summaries.title: Obesogen Holdover: Prenatal Exposure Predicts Cardiometabolic Risk Factors in Childhood.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used the Rhea mother-child cohort, which involved direct blood samples and physical measurements, rather than EHR or linked health data.
summaries.summary: A study investigating the link between prenatal exposure to persistent organic pollutants (POPs) and cardiometabolic risk factors in children aged 4 in Greece.
key_findings: A 10-fold increase in maternal HCB concentrations was associated with higher risks of generalized and abdominal obesity, greater skinfold thickness, higher systolic blood pressure, and a modest increase in child BMI z-score.
Prenatal DDE exposure was associated with higher BMI z-score, increased risk of abdominal obesity, and higher diastolic blood pressure.
PCBs were not associated with any of the risk factors assessed.
captured_features: maternal blood concentrations of DDE, HCB, and PCBs
summaries.population: Nearly 700 mother-child pairs from the Rhea cohort in Greece
limitations: Unclear implications for future health outcomes

summaries.title: Early-Life Exposure to Clostridium leptum Causes Pulmonary Immunosuppression.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the study uses an infant mouse model.
summaries.summary: Study investigating the effects of early-life Clostridium leptum exposure on asthma development in infant mice.
key_findings: Early-life C. leptum exposure increased regulatory T (Treg) cells in cesarean-delivered mice.
Mice exposed to C. leptum + OVA did not develop typical asthma phenotypes (airway hyper-responsiveness, cell infiltration, and Th1, Th2, Th9, Th17 inflammation).
C. leptum exposure induces an immunosuppressive environment in the lung.
captured_features: Th1, Th2, Th9, Th17 cell subsets
summaries.population: Infant mice
summaries.data_availability_statement: All relevant data are within the paper.

summaries.title: Long-term effect of early-life stress from earthquake exposure on working memory in adulthood.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; subjects were enrolled and tested using standardized cognitive tests (BVMT-R and HVLT-R).
summaries.summary: A study investigating the long-term effects of the 1976 Tangshan earthquake on the working memory of adults born in the region.
key_findings: Infant exposure to earthquake-related stress impairs visuospatial memory in adulthood.
Fetuses in the middle and late stages of development are more vulnerable to stress-induced damage.
Education level and early-life earthquake exposure are independent risk factors for reduced visuospatial memory.
summaries.population: 907 subjects born and raised in Tangshan, China

summaries.title: Effectiveness of introducing point of care capillary testing and linking screening with routine appointments for increasing blood lead screening rates of young children: a before-after study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Screening rates were determined from routinely collected service data
summaries.summary: A before-after study evaluating the effectiveness of introducing point-of-care capillary testing and linking blood lead screening with routine child health programs (like immunizations) to increase screening rates in children in Broken Hill, Australia.
key_findings: Annual screening rate increased from 0.39 in 2008 to 0.75 in 2012.
Greatest increases were observed in children aged 11–23 and 48–59 months.
Linking screening with well-accepted mainstream child health programs is an effective strategy to improve participation.
summaries.population: Pre-school aged children in Broken Hill, NSW, Australia

summaries.title: Exposure to grass pollen--but not birch pollen--affects lung function in Swedish children.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses the Swedish birth cohort BAMSE, which involves clinical examinations and blood sampling, but does not mention the use of electronic health records (EHR).
summaries.summary: A study investigating the impact of nine types of pollen and air pollution on lung function in Swedish children aged 8 and 16.
key_findings: Exposure to grass pollen the day before testing was associated with reduced FEV1 in 8-year-olds.
The negative effect of grass pollen was stronger in children sensitized to pollen allergens.
Birch pollen did not show a significant effect on lung function.
summaries.population: Swedish children (8 and 16 years old) in the Stockholm region
limitations: Study limited to the Stockholm region

summaries.title: Effects of 4-nonylphenol on oxidant/antioxidant balance system inducing hepatic steatosis in male rat.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None. The study is an experimental animal study using Sprague-Dawley rats.
summaries.summary: The study investigates the effects of 4-nonylphenol (4-NP) on the oxidant/antioxidant balance and its role in inducing hepatic steatosis in male rats.
key_findings: 4-NP induces hepatic steatosis and apoptosis in rats.
Up-regulated genes involved in apoptosis, hepatotoxicity, and oxidative stress were observed.
Increased ROS and decreased antioxidant enzymes were found in 4-NP exposed rats.
Elevated serum GOT, GPT, LDH, and gamma-GT were observed.

summaries.title: Mitochondrial oxidative DNA damage and exposure to particulate air pollution in mother-newborn pairs.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None mentioned; the study uses data from the ENVIRONAGE birth cohort.
summaries.summary: The study investigated the association between particulate matter (PM) exposure during pregnancy and mitochondrial 8-hydroxy-2'-deoxyguanosine (8-OHdG) levels, a biomarker for oxidative stress, in both mothers and newborns.
key_findings: PM10 exposure during the entire pregnancy was positively associated with maternal 8-OHdG levels.
PM2.5 exposure was significantly associated with increased maternal 8-OHdG levels during the entire pregnancy and third trimester.
PM10 exposure during the first and second trimesters was significantly associated with increased 8-OHdG levels in umbilical cord blood.

limitations: Not specified in the provided text

summaries.title: Is There a Causal Relation between Maternal Acetaminophen Administration and ADHD?
summaries.ehr_evidence: None. The study uses a murine model (CD1 mice).
summaries.summary: An experimental study using a murine model to investigate whether prenatal exposure to acetaminophen (APAP) causes hyperkinetic dysfunction (ADHD-like behavior) in offspring.
key_findings: No significant differences in locomotor activity between APAP-exposed and control mouse offspring.
No differences in volumes of 29 brain areas of interest on MRI.
No differences in neuronal quantifications between groups.

limitations: Lack of accurate assessment of ADHD in murine models
Results should be taken with caution when compared to clinical data

summaries.title: Bisphenol A and Adiposity in an Inner-City Birth Cohort.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses a prospective birth cohort (CCCEH) with urine samples and anthropometric measurements, not EHR.
summaries.summary: A prospective birth cohort study examining the association between prenatal and early-childhood bisphenol A (BPA) exposure and childhood adiposity measures (FMI, %BF, WC) in a New York City birth cohort.
key_findings: Prenatal urinary BPA concentrations were positively associated with child age 7 FMI, %BF, and WC.
Child urinary BPA concentrations were not associated with child anthropometric outcomes.

summaries.title: Moderate Prenatal Cadmium Exposure and Adverse Birth Outcomes: a Role for Sex-Specific Differences?
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used data from the Avon Longitudinal Study of Parents and Children (ALSPAC), which is a population-based cohort study, not an EHR.
summaries.summary: The study investigated the association between moderate prenatal cadmium (Cd) exposure and birth outcomes (birthweight, head circumference, and crown-heel length) in a large cohort of pregnant women, specifically examining whether effects are sex-specific.
key_findings: Maternal blood Cd was adversely associated with birthweight and crown-heel length in the whole group.
In girls, maternal blood Cd was adversely associated with birthweight, head circumference, and crown-heel length, but these associations were not evident in boys.

limitations: Sex-specific effects may mask associations in mixed-sex studies

summaries.title: Effects of low-level prenatal lead exposure on child IQ at 4 and 8 years in a UK birth cohort study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses the ALSPAC birth cohort, which involves blood samples and cognitive testing, not electronic health records.
summaries.summary: Investigation of the association between prenatal lead exposure and child IQ at ages 4 and 8 in a UK birth cohort.
key_findings: No association of prenatal lead exposure with child IQ at 4 or 8 years in adjusted models.
Positive association for IQ at age 8 in girls.
Negative coefficients for IQ at age 8 in boys.

summaries.title: Reduction of in utero lead exposures in South African populations: Positive impact of unleaded petrol.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: The study assessed in utero lead (Pb) exposure and birth outcomes in a South African population following the removal of lead from petrol, measuring Pb in maternal blood, urine, and cord blood.
key_findings: Positive correlation between maternal blood Pb and cord blood Pb (rho = 0.65).
Geographical differences in gestational age, birth length, head circumference, and Apgar scores.
Positive association between cord blood Pb and head circumference in female neonates.
Maternal Pb levels were positively correlated with race, educational status, water sources, cooking fuels, and pesticide use.
summaries.population: Pregnant women and neonates in South Africa
limitations: Study focuses on birth outcomes rather than long-term neurodevelopmental impacts in children.

summaries.ehr_evidence: The study is a prospective birth cohort study using direct specimen collection (blood, hair, breast milk) and face-to-face tests, not EHR or linked health data.
summaries.summary: An update on the Hokkaido Study on Environment and Children’s Health, a prospective birth cohort study in Japan investigating the effects of environmental chemical exposures on birth outcomes, child development, and genetic/epigenetic markers.
key_findings: Environmental chemical exposures demonstrate effects on genetically susceptible populations and on DNA methylation.

summaries.title: Body Burden of Dichlorodiphenyl Dichloroethene (DDE) and Childhood Pulmonary Function.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: Longitudinal study investigating the relationship between blood DDE levels and pulmonary function in children aged 8-10 in Germany, specifically looking at mediation by height and weight.
key_findings: DDE measured at 8 years had significant, inverse, indirect, and total effects on FVC and FEV1 at 10 years, mediated through effects on height and weight.

summaries.title: Proteomic Biomarkers for Bisphenol A-Early Exposure and Women's Thyroid Cancer.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None. The study uses mouse models and clinical samples from patients.
summaries.summary: The study investigates the effects of early-life Bisphenol A (BPA) exposure on thyroid proteins in mice and identifies ANXA6 and VCP as potential proteomic biomarkers associated with thyroid cancer in women.
key_findings: 17 proteins were altered in a dose-dependent manner in the thyroid tissues of offspring mice.
Positive association between blood BPA levels and mRNA expression of ANXA6 and VCP in thyroid cancer tissues (but not in normal tissues).

summaries.ehr_evidence: None mentioned; data was collected via direct anthropometric measurements and water concentration testing.
summaries.summary: A prospective cohort study of 120 infants in arsenic-contaminated areas of Bangladesh to assess the association between household drinking water arsenic exposure and nutritional status (stunting, wasting, and underweight) from birth to 9 months of age.
key_findings: Significant association of stunting with household drinking water arsenic exposure ≥50 μg/L at 9 months of age (p = 0.009).
No significant changes in other nutritional indices (wasting, underweight) over time or with arsenic exposure levels.
captured_features: Arsenic concentration in tube well drinking water
summaries.population: 120 infants (birth to 9 months) in arsenic-contaminated areas of Bangladesh

summaries.title: Low-fidelity Venezuelan equine encephalitis virus polymerase mutants to improve live-attenuated vaccine safety and efficacy.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: Laboratory study investigating low-fidelity mutations in the RNA-dependent RNA-polymerase (RdRp) of the Venezuelan equine encephalitis virus (VEEV) vaccine strain TC-83 to improve vaccine attenuation and immunogenicity.
key_findings: Mutants TC-83 3x and TC-83 4x showed increased immunogenicity and complete protection against lethal challenge in adult mice.
Mutants showed inconsistent attenuation in infant mice.
Low-fidelity mutations may lead to vaccine phenotype instability.
summaries.population: Murine models (infant and adult mice)

summaries.title: Local- and regional-scale air pollution modelling (PM(10)) and exposure assessment for pregnancy trimesters, infancy, and childhood to age 15 years: Avon Longitudinal Study of Parents And Children (ALSPAC).
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses the Avon Longitudinal Study of Parents And Children (ALSPAC) birth cohort and residential address history, not electronic health records.
summaries.summary: The study establishes air pollution modelling (PM10) to assess exposures during pregnancy, infancy, and childhood (up to age 15) for the ALSPAC birth cohort using local and regional/long-range models.
key_findings: PM10 exposures fell from 31.2 μg/m³ at age 1 to 19.6 μg/m³ at age 15.
Local sources contributed 18.5%–19.5% to total PM10 during pregnancy and infancy.
Main roads contributed approximately 3.0% to total PM10 exposures across all periods.
summaries.population: Children and mothers in the ALSPAC birth cohort (pregnancy to age ~15)
limitations: Lack of fixed-site PM10 monitoring during pregnancy
Reliance on dispersion modelling for historic sub-annual periods

summaries.title: Total mercury exposure in early pregnancy has no adverse association with scholastic ability of the offspring particularly if the mother eats fish.

key_findings: No evidence of harm associated with total mercury levels provided the mother ate fish during pregnancy.
Math and science skills were unaffected by maternal mercury levels provided she ate fish.

summaries.summary: A longitudinal study of the PELAGIE birth cohort in France investigating the impact of prenatal occupational exposure to organic solvents on children's internalizing and externalizing behaviors at ages 2 and 6.
key_findings: Increased externalizing behavior at age 2 associated with prenatal solvent exposure.

No association was observed for internalizing behavior.

summaries.title: Analysis By Deep Sequencing of Discontinued Neurotropic Yellow Fever Vaccine Strains.

key_findings: FNV strains show lower polymorphism content than the parental FVV strain.
Heterogeneity and reversion to wild-type identity were observed in FNV strains.
Specific population structures were associated with neurotropic properties.
Adaptation of FNV contributed to the neurotropic adverse phenotype.

key_findings: Intrauterine acetaminophen exposure increased the odds of emotional problems (OR=1.47) and hyperactivity/inattention (OR=1.42) in 6-year-old boys.
No association was observed for girls.
The effect size decreased slightly at age 11.

summaries.population: Children in Brazil (Pelotas birth cohort)

summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used data from the Avon Longitudinal Study of Parents and Children (ALSPAC) birth cohort, specifically whole blood samples and food-frequency questionnaires (FFQ).
summaries.summary: The study evaluated whether dietary patterns and specific food groups are associated with blood lead (B-Pb) concentrations in pregnant women to identify potential dietary factors influencing fetal lead exposure.

Positive association between the food group 'all leafy green and green vegetables' and the likelihood of B-Pb ≥5 µg/dL
Negative association between the food group 'cakes and biscuits' and the likelihood of B-Pb ≥5 µg/dL

summaries.title: Mitotically heritable effects of BMAA on striatal neural stem cell proliferation and differentiation.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: An in vitro laboratory study investigating the direct and mitotically inherited effects of the cyanobacterial toxin BMAA on neural stem cell (NSC) proliferation, differentiation, and morphology.
key_findings: Neural stem cells are more susceptible to BMAA than primary neurons.
250 µM BMAA reduces NSC proliferation through apoptosis and G2/M arrest.
50-100 µM BMAA reduces differentiation of NSCs into astrocytes, oligodendrocytes, and neurons.
BMAA-induced changes are mitotically heritable to daughter cells.

summaries.title: The effect of H1N1 vaccination on serum miRNA expression in children: A tale of caution for microRNA microarray studies.

key_findings: 19 microRNAs were differentially expressed after vaccination in the discovery cohort.
miR-142-3p was downregulated in the discovery cohort but not replicated in the validation cohort.
Many microRNAs detected by microarray could not be validated by RT-qPCR.

limitations: Small sample size in validation cohort (n=22)
Microarray findings were not consistently replicated by RT-qPCR

summaries.title: Repeated holdout validation for weighted quantile sum regression.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses data from the Swedish Environmental Longitudinal, Mother and Child, Asthma and Allergy (SELMA) study, which is a longitudinal cohort study, not an EHR.
summaries.summary: The study proposes a repeated holdout validation method for Weighted Quantile Sum (WQS) regression to assess the impact of chemical mixtures on health outcomes. It demonstrates the method using data from the SELMA study to evaluate the association between prenatal exposure to 26 endocrine disrupting chemicals and child Intelligence Quotient (IQ).
key_findings: Repeated holdout validation improves the stability of WQS estimates in finite study samples.
The WQS index estimate was significant when all data was used but attenuated and nonsignificant using repeated holdout validation.
Repeated holdout can both stabilize results and help characterize the uncertainty in identifying chemicals of concern.
captured_features: Prenatal exposure to 26 endocrine disrupting chemicals
summaries.study_design: Methodological validation using a longitudinal cohort
summaries.population: 718 mother-child pairs in the Swedish Environmental Longitudinal, Mother and Child, Asthma and Allergy (SELMA) study

Mite sensitization risk with Der p 1 exposure was consistently greater in children with FLG mutations.
Significant interaction between FLG and dog ownership, with lower risk of sensitization in children with FLG mutations exposed to dogs in infancy.

summaries.title: BCG Moreau Vaccine Safety Profile and NK Cells-Double Protection Against Disseminated BCG Infection in Retrospective Study of BCG Vaccination in 52 Polish Children with Severe Combined Immunodeficiency.

key_findings: Significantly lower rate of disseminated BCG infections (19% vs 34% in the Marciano study).

summaries.title: Childhood type 1 diabetes: an environment-wide association study across England.
summaries.ehr_evidence: We obtained type 1 diabetes data from National Health Service (NHS) Digital hospital episode statistics (HES) records.
summaries.summary: An ecological environment-wide association study (EnWAS) investigating environmental and demographic risk factors associated with childhood type 1 diabetes incidence in England.
key_findings: 15 out of 53 demographic and environmental risk factors were significantly associated with diabetes incidence.
Air pollutants (PM, NO2, NOx, CO) were inversely associated with incidence.
Significant associations found with lead in soil, radon, outdoor light at night, overcrowding, population density, and ethnicity.
Association found between type 1 diabetes and the living environment domain of the Index of Multiple Deprivation and radon potential class.
summaries.population: children aged 0–9 years in England
summaries.exposure_domain: environmental pollution, meteorological factors, land use, demographic factors
summaries.data_availability_statement: The majority of the demographic and environmental factors included in the study were derived from Crown copyright data and are available via the links provided in the text and references. HES data are available via NHS Digital on application with appropriate ethics and governance permissions; we do not hold data provider, ethics or governance permissions to share these health data with third parties.

summaries.title: Unilateral morning glory disc anomaly in a patient with prenatal Zika virus exposure.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None. The study is a clinical case report.
summaries.summary: A case report describing a 3-year-old male child with unilateral morning glory disc anomaly (MGDA) following prenatal Zika virus (ZIKV) exposure.
key_findings: First report to suggest an association between prenatal ZIKV exposure and MGDA.
Prenatal ZIKV exposure may be associated with a wider pathologic spectrum than previously reported.
summaries.population: 3-year-old male child
summaries.data_availability_statement: All data generated or analyzed during this study are included in this manuscript.

summaries.title: Early-Life Famine Exposure and Risk of Cardiovascular Diseases in Later Life: Findings From the REACTION Study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used data from the REACTION Study, which recruited community-dwelling adults.
summaries.summary: A longitudinal study investigating the association between early-life exposure to the Chinese famine (1959-1962) and the risk of cardiovascular diseases (CVD) in adults.
key_findings: Early-life famine exposure (fetal, childhood, and adolescent) significantly increased the risk of total CVD, myocardial infarction, stroke, and coronary heart disease.
Individuals in severely affected famine areas had a significantly increased risk of total CVD across all three exposed groups.
summaries.population: 259,657 community-dwelling adults aged 40 years or older from mainland China

summaries.title: Long-Term Effects of Environmental Lead on Erythropoietin Production in Young Adults: A Follow-Up Study of a Prospective Cohort in Kosovo.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses a prospective birth cohort and a biorepository, not EHR.
summaries.summary: A follow-up study of a prospective birth cohort in Kosovo to evaluate the long-term effects of prenatal and early childhood lead (Pb) exposure on erythropoietin (EPO) production in young adults.
key_findings: High environmental Pb exposure required increased EPO production to maintain normal Hgb at ages 4.5 and 6.5.
By ages 9.5 and 12, participants were unable to hyper-produce EPO to maintain normal Hgb, suggesting cumulative renal toxicity.
Reduction in Pb exposure may reverse the impact on EPO production.
summaries.population: 101 participants (mean age 24.9 years) from a prospective birth cohort in Kosovo
limitations: Recruitment challenges in Prishtina due to city expansion and demographic changes
summaries.data_availability_statement: All pertinent data are in our study files and can be made available upon request.

summaries.title: Warp Speed for Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19) Vaccines: Why Are Children Stuck in Neutral?
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: A viewpoint article advocating for the immediate initiation of phase 2 clinical trials for COVID-19 vaccines in children to address health, economic, and equity impacts.
key_findings: Pediatric COVID-19 vaccine trials are lagging behind adult trials.
Children face significant hospitalization and mortality risks from SARS-CoV-2.
Racial disparities exist in COVID-19 severity among children.
summaries.population: Children in the United States

summaries.title: DNA methylome signatures of prenatal exposure to synthetic glucocorticoids in hippocampus and peripheral whole blood of female guinea pigs in early life.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: This study investigated the DNA methylation signatures in the hippocampus and whole blood of guinea pig offspring following prenatal exposure to synthetic glucocorticoids (sGC) to assess the concordance of epigenetic programming across different tissues.
key_findings: Over 90% of common methylation signatures associated with sGC exposure showed the same directionality of change.
134 genes were modified in both hippocampus and blood, of which 61 showed methylation changes at identical CpG sites.
Prenatal sGC exposure alters the methylation status of gene clusters involved in brain development.
summaries.population: Female guinea pig offspring
summaries.data_availability_statement: The data discussed in this publication have been deposited in NCBI ’s Gene Expression Omnibus 48 and are accessible through GEO Series accession number GSE163447.

summaries.title: Assessment of chronic bronchitis and risk factors in young adults: results from BAMSE.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; data were obtained from the Swedish BAMSE population-based birth cohort using questionnaire data and clinical measures.
summaries.summary: The study assesses the prevalence and early-life risk factors (e.g., air pollution, breastfeeding) for chronic bronchitis in young adults using the Swedish BAMSE birth cohort.
key_findings: Prevalence of chronic bronchitis (CB) was 5.5%
Daily smoking associated with CB (aOR 3.85)
Air pollution (black carbon at ages 1-4y) associated with CB (aOR 1.71)
Exclusive breastfeeding for >=4 months associated with lower CB risk (aOR 0.66)
summaries.population: Young adults in Sweden
limitations: Not specified in the provided text

summaries.title: Aspartame and cancer - new evidence for causation.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the study uses laboratory animal data from the Ramazzini Institute.
summaries.summary: A re-evaluation of toxicological studies by the Ramazzini Institute confirming that aspartame causes dose-related increases in malignant tumors in rodents, including increased risk in offspring following prenatal exposure.
key_findings: 92.3% of cases confirmed as malignancy
Prenatal exposure to aspartame increases cancer risk in rodent offspring
No evidence of Mycoplasma infection
summaries.population: Rodents (Sprague-Dawley rats and Swiss mice)

summaries.title: Immunogenicity and Safety of AS03-adjuvanted H5N1 Influenza Vaccine in Children 6-35 Months of Age: Results From a Phase 2, Randomized, Observer-blind, Multicenter, Dose-ranging Study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: A phase 2, randomized, observer-blind, multicenter, dose-ranging study evaluating the immunogenicity and safety of different formulations of an AS03-adjuvanted H5N1 influenza vaccine in children aged 6–35 months.
key_findings: All formulations were highly immunogenic.
1.9 µg HA/AS03B provided the most favorable balance of immunogenicity versus reactogenicity.
Antibody levels persisted >4 times above baseline 12 months after primary vaccination with all formulations.
Incidence of fever ranged from 28.6% to 60.5% depending on the formulation.
summaries.study_design: Phase 2, randomized, observer-blind, multicenter, dose-ranging study
summaries.population: children 6–35 months of age

summaries.title: Maternal high-fat diet modifies myelin organization, microglial interactions, and results in social memory and sensorimotor gating deficits in adolescent mouse offspring.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None. The study uses a laboratory mouse model.
summaries.summary: The study investigates the effects of maternal high-fat diet (mHFD) on myelin organization, microglial interactions, and behavioral outcomes in adolescent mouse offspring.
key_findings: mHFD results in neuroinflammation and hypomyelination in offspring
mHFD-exposed male offspring showed increased myelin density with reduced cytosolic myelin channel area in the corpus callosum
lower transcripts of myelination-associated genes (e.g., Igf1) in the hippocampus of male offspring
mHFD-exposed offspring presented loss of social memory and sensorimotor gating deficits
summaries.population: Adolescent mouse offspring

summaries.title: The Rotavirus Vaccine Story: From Discovery to the Eventual Control of Rotavirus Disease.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None. The manuscript is a historical narrative review of rotavirus discovery and vaccine development.
summaries.summary: A historical overview of the discovery of rotavirus, the development of various vaccines (RotaShield, Rotarix, Rotateq, Rotavac, Rotasiil), and their impact on reducing childhood diarrhea and mortality globally.
key_findings: Rotavirus is a leading cause of severe diarrhea and mortality in children under 5 years old.
Vaccines have significantly decreased hospitalizations and deaths from diarrhea in over 100 countries.
Live oral vaccines are less effective in low-income countries for reasons that are not yet understood.
summaries.population: Children under 5 years old globally
summaries.data_availability_statement: Supplementary materials are available at The Journal of Infectious Diseases online. Consisting of data provided by the authors to benefit the reader, the posted materials are not copyedited and are the sole responsibility of the authors, so questions or comments should be addressed to the corresponding author.

summaries.title: Nasal spray live attenuated influenza vaccine: the first experience in Italy in children and adolescents during the 2020-21 season.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Data were collected using questionnaires given on vaccination day and follow-up contacts with parents 1 to 3 months later.
summaries.summary: The study evaluates the first experience of administering the quadrivalent live attenuated influenza vaccine (qLAIV) to children and adolescents in Italy during the 2020–21 season, assessing adverse reactions, satisfaction, and information sources.
key_findings: Adverse events were reported in 24.8% of children, with 80.6% being mild.
Children aged 2–5 years have an increased risk of adverse events compared to older age groups.
High satisfaction levels reported by both parents (83.3%) and healthcare operators (93%).
Word-of-mouth was the primary source of information for parents (39.8%).
summaries.population: 3226 children aged 2–17 years from 2152 families in Italy

summaries.title: The association between in utero exposure to maternal psychological stress and female reproductive function in adolescence: A prospective cohort study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the study uses a population-based pregnancy cohort (The Raine Study) with self-reported questionnaires and clinical measurements.
summaries.summary: A prospective cohort study investigating whether maternal psychological stress (stressful life events) during pregnancy affects reproductive function in female offspring during adolescence.
key_findings: Maternal stressful life events in late gestation were associated with greater uterine volume and higher ovarian antral follicle count (AFC) in adolescent offspring.
No associations were found between maternal stress and circulating AMH or Inhibin B.
summaries.population: 228 female offspring (age 14-16 years)

summaries.title: Early life exposure to house dust mite allergen prevents experimental allergic asthma requiring mitochondrial H(2)O(2).
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study is an experimental animal model using mice, not human electronic health records.
summaries.summary: An experimental study investigating the role of mitochondrial H2O2 in establishing airway tolerance to house dust mite (HDM) allergens in mice.
key_findings: Early-life exposure to low-dose HDM prevents allergic airway inflammation in later life.
Depletion of mitochondrial H2O2 in MCAT mice abrogates HDM-induced airway tolerance.
Mitochondrial H2O2 plays a role in constraining lung dendritic cell numbers to establish early-life airway tolerance.
summaries.population: Newborn and young mice
summaries.data_availability_statement: Associated DataSupplementary Materials1

summaries.title: Characterisation of lung function trajectories and associated early-life predictors in an Australian birth cohort study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses data from the Raine Study, a prospective pregnancy cohort, rather than an EHR or linked health database.
summaries.summary: A longitudinal study characterizing lung function trajectories (FEV1, FVC, FEV1/FVC) from 6 to 22 years of age in an Australian birth cohort to identify early-life risk factors.
key_findings: Four lung function trajectories identified for FEV1, FVC, and FEV1/FVC.
Asthma, maternal smoking, and early-life PM2.5 exposure were identified as risk factors for low lung function trajectories.
summaries.population: 1512 children from the Raine Study (Western Australia)
limitations: Specific to a single birth cohort
summaries.data_availability_statement: Supplementary material 00072-2022.SUPPLEMENT Supplementary material 00072-2022.SUPPLEMENTARY_CLEAN_FS

summaries.title: Vaccine-Associated Measles Encephalitis in Immunocompromised Child, California, USA.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None. The manuscript is a single case report and does not mention the use of Electronic Health Records (EHR) or linked health data.
summaries.summary: A case report of a fatal vaccine-associated measles encephalitis in an immunocompromised child in California, USA, who developed the infection following MMR ProQuad vaccination and a stem cell transplant.

Observed biased matrix-gene hypermutation consistent with persistent measles virus central nervous system infection.

summaries.title: Prenatal exposure to insecticides and child cardiometabolic risk factors in the VHEMBE birth cohort.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used peripartum samples collected from a birth cohort (VHEMBE) rather than electronic health records.

Maternal p,p’-DDT concentrations were associated with a reduction in body fat percentage.

summaries.title: COVID-19 mRNA Vaccine-Associated Myocarditis Presenting as STEMI in a 48-Year-Old Male.

key_findings: Patient presented with STEMI-like symptoms and elevated troponins post-vaccination.
Coronary angiography revealed normal coronary arteries.
Symptoms and diagnostic markers resolved with medical therapy.

summaries.title: Early-life exposure to hardship increased risk tolerance and entrepreneurship in adulthood with gender differences.
summaries.ehr_evidence: No EHR data mentioned; the study uses historical and demographic data related to China's Great Famine.
summaries.summary: The study investigates the impact of early-life hardship (specifically China's Great Famine) on adult risk tolerance and entrepreneurship, exploring gender differences and behavioral mechanisms.
key_findings: Individuals who experienced more hardship were more likely to become entrepreneurs.
Hardship was associated with greater risk tolerance in both men and women.
Hardship increased business ownership only among men.
The increase in entrepreneurship was at least partly due to conditioning rather than selection.
captured_features: famine intensity (proxied by population loss from weather shocks)
summaries.population: Adults who experienced China's Great Famine (1959-1961)

summaries.title: Willingness toward COVID-19 vaccination, coadministration with other vaccines and receive a COVID-19 vaccine booster: a cross-sectional study on the guardians of children in China.

Most reports related to 'General Disorders and Administration Site Conditions' (52.3%)

estimated family-income and home market-value were not significantly different

summaries.title: Maternal use of methamphetamine induces sex-dependent changes in myocardial gene expression in adult offspring.

summaries.title: Quantile regression to examine the association of air pollution with subclinical atherosclerosis in an adolescent population.
