summaries.summary: Case report of a 58-year-old female who presented with acute appendicitis approximately 48 hours after receiving the first dose of the Pfizer-BioNTech COVID-19 vaccine.
key_findings: Acute appendicitis occurred 48 hours after first dose of Pfizer-BioNTech vaccine.
Histology showed Th-1 driven granulomatous inflammation.
summaries.population: 58-year-old female
summaries.data_availability_statement: Data are available upon reasonable request to the corresponding author.

summaries.title: COVID-19 Vaccination Responses with Different Vaccine Platforms in Patients with Inborn Errors of Immunity.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used laboratory assays (ELISA, ELISPOT, and flow cytometry) on a clinical cohort rather than electronic health records.
summaries.summary: The study evaluated the safety and immunogenicity of various COVID-19 vaccines (Sputnik, AstraZeneca, Sinopharm, Moderna, and Pfizer) in patients with inborn errors of immunity (IEI) in Argentina.
key_findings: No moderate/severe vaccine-associated adverse events were observed.
Anti-S/RBD titers and neutralizing antibodies were significantly lower in IEI patients compared to age-matched healthy controls.
Moderna vaccines elicited a stronger humoral response in the pediatric population.
84.5% of IEI patients showed a positive S-specific IFN-γ response.
captured_features: S-specific circulating T follicular-helper cells (TFH) frequency
summaries.population: 118 adults and pediatric patients with inborn errors of immunity (IEI) and 37 age-matched healthy controls in Argentina

summaries.title: Open-label phase I/II clinical trial of SARS-CoV-2 receptor binding domain-tetanus toxoid conjugate vaccine (FINLAY-FR-2) in combination with receptor binding domain-protein vaccine (FINLAY-FR-1A) in children.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the study is a clinical trial.
summaries.summary: A phase I/II open-label clinical trial evaluating the safety and immunogenicity of a heterologous SARS-CoV-2 vaccine scheme (FINLAY-FR-2 and FINLAY-FR-1A) in children aged 3-18 in Havana, Cuba.
key_findings: Local pain was the only adverse event with frequency > 10% and was not serious.
Two doses of FINLAY-FR-2 followed by a third dose of FINLAY-FR-1A increased the humoral immune response in all children.
Neutralizing titers were measured against Alpha, Delta, Beta, and Omicron variants.
summaries.population: 350 children 3-18 years old in Havana, Cuba

summaries.title: Associations of longitudinal BMI percentile classification patterns in early childhood with neighborhood-level social determinants of health.
summaries.ehr_evidence: We extracted EHR data from 2012-2019 for a children’s health system that includes 2 hospitals and wide network of outpatient clinics spanning 5 East Coast states in the US.
summaries.summary: The study identifies distinct longitudinal BMI-percentile classification groups in children aged 0-7 years and examines their associations with 27 neighborhood-level social determinants of health (SDOH) using EHR data.
key_findings: Five BMI-percentile classification groups emerged: always obesity, overweight most of the time, increasing BMI-percentile, decreasing BMI-percentile, and always normal weight.
Children in the obesity/overweight clusters were more likely to live in neighborhoods with higher poverty, unemployment, crowded households, single-parent households, and lower preschool enrollment.
summaries.study_design: Retrospective cohort study using data-driven and algorithmic clustering and multinomial logistic regression
summaries.population: 36,910 children aged 0-7 years in a children's health system across 5 East Coast states in the US

summaries.title: Correction: Neonatal blood lead concentration predicts medium term lead-related outcomes in children ≤5 years old with congenital lead poisoning: A retrospective cohort study in Northern Nigeria.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None mentioned in the correction notice.
summaries.summary: Correction notice for a retrospective cohort study investigating how neonatal blood lead concentration predicts medium-term outcomes in children under 5 years old with congenital lead poisoning in Northern Nigeria.
summaries.population: children <5 years old with congenital lead poisoning in Northern Nigeria

summaries.title: Association between prenatal exposure to alkylphenols and intelligence quotient among preschool children: sex-specific effects.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses data from the Guangxi Zhuang Birth Cohort, involving maternal serum measurements and Wechsler Preschool and Primary Scale of the Intelligence scores, rather than electronic health records.
summaries.summary: This study explores the association between prenatal exposure to alkylphenols (APs) and the intelligence quotient (IQ) of preschool children, examining sex-specific and nonmonotonic effects.
key_findings: Prenatal exposure to nonylphenol (NP) and 4-tert-octylphenol (4-T-OP) was inversely associated with full-scale IQ (FSIQ) and working memory index (WMI).
Prenatal exposure to 4-n-nonylphenol (4-N-NP) showed a positive association with the fluid reasoning index in girls.
A U-shaped relationship was observed between maternal 4-T-OP and WMI in total children and girls.
Combined AP mixtures above the 50th percentile exhibited an inverse trend on FSIQ in total children and girls.
captured_features: maternal serum concentrations of NP, 4-T-OP, 4-N-NP, and 4-n-octylphenol
childhood IQ scores (FSIQ, WMI, fluid reasoning index)
summaries.population: 221 mother-child pairs from the Guangxi Zhuang Birth Cohort (preschool children aged 3-6 years)
limitations: Specific to a single birth cohort

summaries.title: Activated fibroblasts modify keratinocyte stem niche through TET1 and IL-6 to promote their rapid transformation in a mouse model of prenatal arsenic exposure.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: Study investigating how prenatal arsenic exposure activates dermal fibroblasts to modify the keratinocyte stem cell niche, promoting skin tumorigenesis in a mouse model.
key_findings: Prenatal arsenic exposure increases activation markers (α-SMA, Fibronectin, Collagen IV) in dermal fibroblasts.
As-conditioned fibroblasts induce TET1 and secrete high levels of IL-6.
IL-6 promotes EMT, proliferation, and migration in keratinocyte stem cells via JAK2-STAT3 signaling.
Inhibition of pSTAT3 reduces EMT and KSC proliferation.
summaries.population: BALB/c mouse offspring
summaries.data_availability_statement: The datasets generated and analyzed during the current study are submitted to CSIR-IITR repository and will be made available by corresponding author on request.

summaries.title: Genetic exploration of Dravet syndrome: two case report.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: A case series of two Indonesian children with Dravet syndrome, focusing on genetic analysis using next-generation sequencing to identify mutations in the SCN1A gene and other genes.
key_findings: Patient 1 had heterozygous deletion mutations in TTC21B, SCN1A, and SCN9A.
Patient 2 had a mutation in the SCN1A gene.
Clinical severity correlated with genetic mutation severity.
summaries.population: Two Indonesian children

summaries.title: Comparison of 1 Versus 2 Doses of Quadrivalent Influenza Vaccine in 3-8-Year-Old Children with Different Immunological States - Jiangsu Province, China, 2021.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: A clinical trial assessing the immunogenicity and safety of one versus two doses of the quadrivalent influenza vaccine (QIV) in children aged 3–8 years in China, stratified by previous vaccination history.
key_findings: Evidence supports a single dose of QIV for children aged 3–8 years who have previously received two or more doses of influenza vaccine.
Children who have not been previously vaccinated should still adhere to the recommended schedule of receiving two doses.
summaries.population: healthy children aged 3–8 years, Jiangsu Province, China
summaries.data_availability_statement: Supplementary data to this article can be found online.

summaries.title: The Epidemiological Characteristics and Spatiotemporal Clustering of Measles - China, 2005-2022.
summaries.ehr_evidence: We utilized data from the China Disease Prevention and Control Information System.
summaries.summary: A spatiotemporal analysis of measles epidemiology in China from 2005 to 2022 to identify trends, hotspots, and high-risk populations.
key_findings: 732,218 measles cases were reported between 2005 and 2022.
The incidence rate reached a historical low of 0.039 per 100,000 in 2022.
A high-incidence cluster was identified from 2005 to 2008 in the western, central, and northern regions of China.
Measles predominantly affects young children.
summaries.population: Children in China
limitations: Relies on reported cases from a surveillance system

summaries.title: Association between prenatal glucocorticoid exposure and adolescent neurodevelopment: An observational follow-up study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study is a prospective observational follow-up using direct clinical measurements (IQ tests, EEG, stress paradigms) rather than EHR or linked health data.
summaries.summary: A prospective observational follow-up study investigating the long-term effects of prenatal betamethasone exposure on neurodevelopmental, metabolic, and autonomic outcomes in adolescents.
key_findings: No statistically significant group difference in global IQ, ADHD symptoms, metabolic markers, HPAA activity, or ANS activity.
Reduced electrocortical activity in the frontal brain region in the betamethasone group.
summaries.population: 21 adolescents (14-15 years old) exposed to prenatal betamethasone; 35 reference children
summaries.data_availability_statement: Additional supporting information can be found online in the Supporting Information section at the end of this article.

summaries.title: Newborn glomerular function and gestational particulate air pollution.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses the ENVIRONAGE cohort and a Dutch questionnaire; no EHR or linked health data are mentioned.
summaries.summary: The study evaluates the impact of gestational particulate air pollution exposure (black carbon and PM2.5) on cord blood cystatin C levels, which serve as a marker for glomerular function in newborns.
key_findings: Every 0.5 μg/m³ and 5 μg/m³ increment in gestational exposure to black carbon (BC) and fine particulate matter (PM2.5) corresponded to increases of 0.04 mg/L and 0.07 mg/L in cord blood cystatin C levels, respectively (p < 0.01).
Third-trimester exposure showed similar associations with increased cystatin C levels.
summaries.population: 1484 mother-child pairs in Genk, Belgium

summaries.title: Associations between gestational exposure to perfluoroalkyl substances, fetal growth, and the mediation effect of thyroid hormones.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used data from the Ezhou birth cohort study, which is a primary cohort study.
summaries.summary: The study investigates the associations between maternal serum PFAS concentrations and neonatal growth metrics, specifically examining the mediating role of thyroid hormones (FT3 and FT4).
key_findings: PFNA and PFHxS were negatively associated with birth length.
PFOA and PFHxS were negatively associated with birth head circumference.
FT3 mediated 36.7% of the negative association between PFNA and birth length.
FT4 mediated 30.8% of the effect of PFOA on head circumference.
summaries.population: 374 mother-infant pairs from the Ezhou birth cohort study (2019-2020)
summaries.data_availability_statement: The datasets used and/or analysed during the current study available from the corresponding author on reasonable request.

summaries.title: Early Life Exposure to Manure-Fertilized Soil Shapes the Gut Antibiotic Resistome.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None. This is an experimental animal study.
summaries.summary: An experimental study investigating how early life exposure to manure-fertilized soil (red and black soils) shapes the gut antibiotic resistome in mice.
key_findings: Manure fertilization significantly enriched tetracycline-resistant ARGs in both red and black soils.
Mice in manure-fertilized environments harbored markedly higher abundances of ARGs, particularly the tet(Q) gene.
Muribaculaceae and Bacteroidaceae were identified as key hosts of tet(Q).
Evidence suggests horizontal gene transfer between Muribaculaceae and Bacteroidaceae.
summaries.population: Mice (laboratory animals)
limitations: Animal model (mice) may not perfectly replicate human gut microbiome dynamics
summaries.data_availability_statement: The raw metagenomic sequence data are publicly available under the accession number PRJNA1206751.

summaries.title: A comprehensive atlas of the bonobo gut bacteriome and its associated host and exposome factors.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses fecal samples and host/exposome data from wild and zoo-housed bonobos, not EHR.
summaries.summary: A comprehensive atlas of the gut bacteriome and associated host and exposome factors in 212 wild and zoo-housed bonobos (Pan paniscus).
key_findings: Differences in gut bacterial diversity between wild and captive bonobos.
Social contact is a stronger predictor of bacteriome similarity than genetics or other exposome factors.
Maternal effects persist even in non-cohabiting mother-offspring dyads.
summaries.population: 212 wild and zoo-housed bonobos (Pan paniscus)
summaries.exposure_domain: Exposome (diet, medication use, social contact, early life adversity, transit time)
summaries.data_availability_statement: Sequencing data have been deposited at the European Nucleotide Archive under project ENA: PRJEB86099 and are publicly available as of the date of publication. Pseudonymized sample data have been deposited and are publicly available as of the data of publication at Zenodo: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.14913431. All original code has been deposited and is publicly available as of the date of publication at Zenodo: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.14911049.

summaries.title: Using machine learning to predict future foster care admission.
summaries.ehr_evidence: We use claims data for children enrolled in a Medicaid health plan in Ohio as well as for linked adults, along with data on individual and geographic social determinants of health (SDOH) factors.
summaries.summary: A study using machine learning to predict foster care admission in Ohio children by analyzing Medicaid claims data and social determinants of health (SDOH).
key_findings: Gradient-boosted tree model identified 2408 children at risk of foster care admission.
SDOH data significantly improved predictive accuracy (PPV of 84.72% compared to 27.44% without SDOH).
summaries.population: children enrolled in a Medicaid health plan in Ohio
limitations: ethical and legal concerns regarding the use of such tools for selecting families for child welfare investigations

summaries.title: Moderate prenatal alcohol exposure differentially alters acute ethanol sensitivity of GABAergic transmission in CRFR1- and CRFR1+ CeM Neurons.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: This study investigates how moderate prenatal alcohol exposure (PAE) alters acute ethanol modulation of GABAergic neurotransmission in the central amygdala (CeM) of adult rats, specifically looking at CRFR1+ and CRFR1- neurons.
key_findings: PAE dysregulates acute EtOH's modulation of GABA transmission within the CeM in a sex-specific and cell-type-dependent manner.
Long-term PAE effects in the CeM are sex-specific.
summaries.population: Adult male and female rats

summaries.title: Sugar restriction in the first 1000 days after conception, and long-term respiratory health: a quasi-experiment study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Outcomes included incident asthma, COPD, and IPF (ascertained by health records)
summaries.summary: A quasi-experimental study investigating the long-term effects of early-life sugar restriction (based on UK sugar rationing) on adult respiratory health, including asthma, COPD, and IPF, and lung function.
key_findings: Early-life sugar rationing associated with lower risks of asthma (HR 0.75) and COPD (HR 0.73)
Delayed disease onset by ≤ 3.6 years
Rationed participants had higher mean FEV1%, FVC%, and FEV1/FVC ratio
Diabetes and hypertension mediated 18.2% of the effect
summaries.population: 58,670 UK Biobank participants born 1951–1956
summaries.data_availability_statement: The data are accessible through a public, open-access repository . Data from the UKB, ELSA, and HRS are available to researchers on request.

summaries.title: Impact of maternal fasting blood glucose in pregnancy on body composition, anthropometric, and metabolic outcomes in newborns and 3-Year-Old offspring: results from the lifestyle in pregnancy and offspring study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used data from the Lifestyle in Pregnancy (LiP) study and its 3-year follow-up, the LiPO study, which are research cohorts.
summaries.summary: This study investigated whether maternal fasting glucose during pregnancy predicts offspring body composition, anthropometry, and metabolic outcomes at birth and at 3 years of age.
key_findings: Higher maternal fasting glucose was associated with a higher birth weight z-score.
At 3 years, no significant associations were found for BMI z-score or metabolic markers.
Glucose levels ≥ 5.6 mmol/L predicted higher birth weight.
summaries.population: 301 women in LiP study, 157 in LiPO study, 75 children with DXA assessments
limitations: Small sample size for DXA assessments (n=75)
No significant associations found at 3 years for primary metabolic markers
summaries.data_availability_statement: The data that support the findings of this study are available from C.A.V., but restrictions apply to their availability. The data were used under license for the current study and include unpublished results that are still being analyzed. Therefore, they are not publicly available. However, data may be obtained from the corresponding author on reasonable request and with permission from C.A.V.

summaries.title: Maternal exposure to cyclophosphamide during prepubertal life does not affect the cryosusceptibility of in vitro derived mouse embryos.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses a mouse model, not human electronic health records.
summaries.summary: A mouse model study evaluating whether prepubertal exposure to the chemotherapy drug cyclophosphamide (CY) affects the cryosusceptibility and developmental quality of embryos derived via in vitro fertilization (IVF).
key_findings: No significant differences were found between CY-exposed and control groups in terms of post-warming survival or pluripotency marker expression.
summaries.population: Mouse model
